1. CSI  Computer Society of India
Vision: "IT for Masses"
Keeping in mind the interest of IT professionals and computer users, CSI works towards making the profession an
area of choice amongst all sections of society. CSI regularly organizes conferences, conventions, lectures, projects,
and awards. It also provides training for physically challenged citizens ('Punarjani') and assists governments in
formulating IT strategy and planning.
Region VII Covers:
Tamil Nadu, Pondicherry, Andaman and Nicobar, Kerala, Lakshadweep.
Kanchipuram Chapter
The Society has developed a well-established network across the country. Activities for students include lecture
meetings, seminars, conferences, training programs, programming contests, and industrial visits. CSI's Educational
Directorate undertakes certification of professionals on the latest technologies and offers distance education in
Business Domain areas.
Awards Received:
(cid:127) College received Best Supporting College (SBC) award from CSI Kancheepuram Chapter
(cid:127) Faculty Mr. D. Weslin received Active Participant Award (SBC) from CSI Kancheepuram Chapter

2. IEEE  Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
The IEEE is a United States-based non-profit organization and the world's leading professional association for the
advancement of technology. It covers areas ranging from aerospace systems, electrical, electronics, computers,
telecommunications, biomedical engineering, electric power, and consumer electronics. IEEE publishes technical
literature and highly qualified research articles across all engineering domains.
Student Branch Activities:
(cid:127) Participation in regional conferences, workshops and competitions
(cid:127) Development of leadership, interpersonal and team building skills
(cid:127) Scholarships and project/design programs and student paper contests
(cid:127) Establishment of a Student Branch Library with IEEE publications
(cid:127) Access to IEEE online services and resources; establish a website
(cid:127) Organize various awareness programs for school students and public

3. IETE  Institution of Electronics and Telecommunication
Engineers
IETE is one of the leading Professional Societies in India for Electronics and Communication Engineers. MSAJCE
established its IETE Students Forum on 16/08/2017 with 100 active members. The forum organizes guest lectures,
seminars, workshops, and conferences. Student members benefit from technical visits, practical training, and project
work in R&D; laboratories and industries.
IETE Publications:
(cid:127) IETE Journal of Education
(cid:127) IETE Journal of Research
(cid:127) IETE Technical Review
Branch Counselors:
(cid:127) Branch Counselor 1: Dr. I. Manju
(cid:127) Branch Counselor 2: Mrs. S. Anusuya
Goals of IETE:
(cid:127) Improving the standard of education
(cid:127) Train students in emerging areas to meet new opportunities
(cid:127) Encouraging outside-the-classroom studies, practical work and seminars
(cid:127) Better employment of students on completion of their courses
(cid:127) Substantial increase of IETE membership and nationwide distribution
Benefits of ISF (IETE Students Forum):
(cid:127) Priority for publishing papers in Journal of Education
(cid:127) Can subscribe to IETE Journal of Research and IETE Technical Review (available online)
(cid:127) ISF students can attend IETE technical programs in India at reduced registration fees
(cid:127) ISF members are issued with an Identity Card

4. SAE  Society of Automotive Engineers
SAE is a US-based, globally active professional association and standards developing organization for engineering
professionals. Principal emphasis is placed on global transport industries such as aerospace, automotive, and
commercial vehicles. SAEINDIA is India's leading resource for mobility technology, operating across four sections:
Northern, Eastern, Southern, and Western.

5. ISHRAE  Indian Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air
Conditioning Engineers
ISHRAE was founded in 1981 at New Delhi by a group of eminent HVAC&R; professionals. It operates from 41
chapters and sub-chapters spread all over India, with HQ in Delhi. ISHRAE works with various Govt.
Ministries/Departments including BIS, BEE (ECBC), MoEFCC (Ozone Cell), and NCCD (Ministry of Agriculture). It is
looked upon as a repository of technical knowledge in the HVAC&R; and Building Industry field by peer organizations
and the Government of India.
Focus Areas:
(cid:127) Sustainability
(cid:127) Green Buildings
(cid:127) Energy Efficiency
(cid:127) Environmental Responsibility
(cid:127) Indoor Air Quality
(cid:127) Fire & Safety
Student Branch Activities:
(cid:127) Excellent networking opportunities to interact with professionals, industry leaders, and key decision makers
(cid:127) Forum for professional development and continuous training on fundamentals and latest technologies
(cid:127) Easy access to all ISHRAE Technical Publications, Software, Workshops, Conferences and Training Programs
(cid:127) Opportunity to work in Chapter Working Committee and Headquarter activities
(cid:127) Platform for effective marketing and launching of new products through exhibitions and advertisements
(cid:127) International exposure through International Associates
